options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: set_lang
options.description:
Select which language of items and locations to send to the multiworld (not the version of the game to be played)
en- English
jp- 日本語

options.name: spoiler_items_in_english
options.description:
Only considered if Set Language is JP (not saved to slot data)
Spoiler log shows progression with English Names

options.name: playthrough_method
options.description:
Select how you would like the game to progress storywise

Linear - Vanilla playthrough, go through the chapters in linear order
Open World - Most things can be done in any order as long as requirements are met to unlock

options.name: skip_over_bosses
options.description:
PLACEHOLDER DOESN'T DO ANYTHING YET
Only used when set to a linear playthrough

Allows starting the next chapter without killing the crest guardian (can be fought later)

options.name: goal
options.description:
Select which Goal in order to win this game of Brave Fencer Musashi

Rescue All NPCs - save all 35 Castle NPCs lost in the multiworld 
Rescue [X] NPCs - save a portion of Castle NPCs, check 'NPCs Required to Goal' to adjust count (does not include the 5 who were not kidnapped)
Defeat [ ] Guardian/Boss - Win after defeating the respective boss (listed in order of occurrence)
Defeat [X] Guardian - Win after defeating a specific number of crest guardians check 'GuardianGoal' to adjust count (helps somewhat with a certain length for open world settings)

options.name: npc_goal
options.description:
How many Castle NPCs will need to be rescued in order to goal. Only relevant when 'Rescue X NPCs' is set as the goal.
Does not include the 5 who were not kidnapped
10 - very very short playthrough possibly all in sphere 1 (<30 minutes solo)
20 - short playthrough (1-3 hours solo)
35 - same as rescue all NPCs (4-7 hours solo)

options.name: guardian_goal
options.display_name: Crest Guardians Required to be Defeated to Goal
options.description:
How many Crest Guardians will need to be defeated in order to goal. Only relevant when 'Defeat X Crest Guardian' is set as the goal.
If playthrough method is set to linear 1 would be the same as defeating earth crest guardian
If playthrough method is set to open world 1 would be the first crest guardian defeated

options.name: force_soda_fountain_last
options.display_name: Force Soda Foutain to be Last
options.description:
True - Soda Fountain will only be considered logically accessible if all other required bosses (by goal setting) are defeated first
       Defeat [Earth/Water/Fire/Wind] Crest Guardian - Soda Fountain is never considered accessible until after goal
       Defeat [Sky/Final Boss] - This setting does nothing
       Rescue NPCs - Soda Fountain is only in logic after defeating Earth, Water, Fire, and Wind Crest Guardians
       Defeat X Crest Guardians - Sky Crest Guardian can be the final Crest Guardian in logic (Defeat 1 Crest Guardian means Sky Crest Guardian could be your first Crest Guardian)
False - Soda Fountain can be expected to be completed prior to defeating other bosses

options.name: starting_hp
options.description:
Amount of max hp to start the game with (max hp will never go over 500)
Setting max hp to 1 will remove all max hp up from itempool (and disable deathlink)

options.name: starting_bp
options.description: Amount of max bp to start the game with (max bp will never go over 500)

options.name: max_hp_logic
options.display_name: Max Hp Logic for Soda Fountain
options.description: Amount of max hp expected logically before going to Soda Fountain (ignored if starting max hp is 1)

options.name: lumina_randomzied
options.description: Randomize the sword of legend

options.name: bakery_sanity
options.description: Randomize the bakery's list of items for sale into the multiworld

options.name: restaurant_sanity
options.description: Randomize the restaurant's list of items for sale into the multiworld

options.name: grocery_sanity
options.description: Randomize the grocery's list of items for sale into the multiworld

options.name: grocery_s_revive
options.description: Force S-Revive to always show up in the shop, collecting a S-Revive from the multiworld will give a price discount

options.name: grocery_sanity_heal_logic
options.display_name: Grocery Sanity Healing Item Combat Logic
options.description: Require either C-Drink or W-Gel logically before being required to defeat a Crest Guardian (only relevant when Grocery Sanity is 'true')

options.name: toy_sanity
options.description: Randomize the toy shop's list of items for sale into the multiworld, only includes Series 1 through 5 (Does not require Soda Fountain)

options.name: tech_sanity
options.description: Randomize the Weapon Techniques and Fusion upgrade into the multiworld

options.name: scroll_sanity
options.description: Randomize the Five Legendary Scrolls into the multiworld

options.name: wind_scroll_logic
options.description:
Only Considered when Scroll Sanity is On. Changes what is considered in logic when Wind Scroll is available

Vanilla - Using Wind Scroll is only required for traversal that it is needed in Vanilla logic
Simple - Wind Scroll jump maybe required in areas that need double jump height and are easy to do
Complex - Wind Scroll jump maybe rquired in areas that need double jump height and have precise jumping timings or landings require swapping scrolls (still need double jump for everything Soda Fountain)
          Wind Scroll may also be expected to use as a replacement in some boss battles

options.name: sky_scroll_logic
options.description:
Only Considered when Scroll Sanity is On. Changes what is considered in logic when Sky Scroll is available

Vanilla - Sky Scroll is not considered, some easy to reach checks may show as out of logic
Simple - Sky Scroll maybe expected to be used to cross gaps or go up a certain slope
Complex - Sky Scroll maybe expected to be used to access areas early or out of order (may expect player to softlock if needed to reach a location [Save often])

options.name: core_sanity
options.description: Randomize the first four elemental Boss Cores needed to activate the elemental crests into the multiworld

options.name: level_sanity
options.description: Randomize the stats gained from leveling into the multiworld

options.name: level_bundles
options.display_name: Number of bundles for combat stats
options.description:
Number of bundles to receive lvl 30 stats (Body, Mind, Fusion, Lumina)
Lowering number bundles will leave more room for Traps/Filler
Only active if Level Sanity is True

options.name: stat_gain_modifier
options.description:
change how stats are gained
Early - Most stats are gained in the first couple bundles (maybe better for shorter goals)
Vanilla - Stats are gained roughly the same rate as the base game
Enhanced - Stats start similar to Vanilla but dramatically increase after receiving half of the stat up bundles (may make Wind Crest Guardian and path to Sky Crest Guardian easier)
Only active if Level Sanity is True

options.name: xp_gain
options.description:
Amount to multiply XP receieved, higher multiplier is faster leveling. Leveling may be required for progression so it is recommended to have increased XP gain.
No XP Gain - Challenge run modifier (level 1 the entire game), checks for leveling are removed (if enabled)

options.name: xp_gain_mind
options.description:
Amount to multiply XP receieved for Mind, higher multiplier is faster leveling. Mind is gained by taking steps which can be a slow process to get to max level. Consider a high XP gain overall or at least a higher xp gain for Mind
defaults to the same multiplier chosen in xp_gain

options.name: quest_item_sanity
options.description: Randomize the items needed to progress the story (i.e. Jon's Key, Logs, Steamwood handles, etc.)

options.name: bp_sanity
options.description: Randomize the stats gained from BP gain (binchos and boss cores) into the multiworld

options.name: bp_bundles
options.display_name: Number of bundles for BP Up
options.description:
Number of bundles to receive BP Up
Lowering number bundles will leave more room for Traps/Filler
Only active if BP Sanity is True

options.name: time_sanity
options.description:
Randomize the flow of time into the multiworld
You always start with Monday from 09:00 to 10:00
This will mean that you will need to unlock a period of time that the Grocery is open to get hp healing items

options.name: time_sanity_settings
options.description:
Separate - days and hours are seperate items (aka Tuesday, Wednesday, 10:00, 18:00, etc.)[adds 31 locations]
Combined - days and hours are combined (aka Tuesday 10:00, Wednesday 18:00, etc.)[adds 169 locations]

options.name: early_skullpion
options.description: Add the NPCs required for Skullpion, Lumina (if randomized), and Bracelet to the early generation (Will likely place in Sphere 1, this setting is only recommended for multiworld generations to avoid being stuck in chapter 2)

options.name: boulder_chase_zoom
options.description: slightly change zoom level during Chapter 1 boulder chase sequence

options.name: leno_sniff_modifier
options.description:
Modify how long Leno spends sniffing when attempting to find the path to the graveyard
10 -> 10% of the vanilla time
100 -> 100% vanilla
200 -> 200% spend twice the time

options.name: skip_minigame_follow_leno
options.description:
Skip over the minigame follow Leno through meandering forest to get to the graveyard
Going into meandering forest with Leno will instead take you directly to the graveyard

options.name: raft_hp
options.description: How many logs to start with when doing the rafting minigame

options.name: raft_difficulty
options.description:
Adjust the difficulty for the rafting minigame
Vanilla - No adjustments
Invincible - Never lose logs
Regrow Logs - Regain logs after a period of time

options.name: raft_regrow
options.description:
Time before regrowing a log during rafting (needs difficulty set to regrow logs to work)
# -> time in seconds before regrowing a log
logs that are regained are currently invisible

options.name: steamwood_timer
options.description:
Modify how fast the 24 hour timer for Steamwood ticks down (both first and second visit)
1 -> 1% of the vanilla time (roughly IRL time)
100 -> 100% vanilla
150 -> 150% timer ticks down 50% faster

options.name: steamwood_valve_timer
options.description:
Modify the starting timer inbetween valves for Steamwood ticks down (both first and second visit)
90 -> 90% of the vanilla time to complete the next valve (harder)
100 -> 100% vanilla
165 -> 165% timer starts 65% higher

options.name: steamwood_disable_countdown
options.description: Whether to disable the countdown for the inbetween valve timer

options.name: steamwood_number_valves
options.description:
The number of valves to be closed to finish steamwood. In the vanilla game you need to complete all 8 but reducing this number will skip over numbers
If set to 1 only valve 8 will need to be completed, otherwise starts on valve 1 and finishes on valve 8

options.name: steamwood_random_valves
options.description: Whether to randomize valve order or not. Randomized on entering steamwood. The lowest number valve that has a red light is the next valve to be completed.

options.name: steamwood_pressure_rise_rate
options.description:
Adjust the rate of pressure gain on some valves
Vanilla - No adjustments
Faster - Valves 1 through 4 will have the same pressure rise as vanilla valve 5
Slower - Valves 5 through 8 will have slightly lower pressure rise
Even - All valves have the same pressure rise as vanilla valve 4

options.name: steamwood_progress_lost
options.description:
The amount of progress lost when confirming while in the red range
-96 -> all progress lost
-16 -> vanilla progress lost
>0 -> gain progress instead

options.name: steamwood_width_of_ok_pressure
options.description:
The width in pixels of acceptable range for pressure
10 - slightly narrower acceptable range
12 - vanilla
96 - the entire range is green

options.name: steamwood_valve_progress_modifier
options.description:
Modify the amount of progressed gained when selecting within the acceptable pressure range
50 -> 50% gain half progress when succeeding
100 -> 100% vanilla
200 -> 200% gain double progress when succeeding

options.name: steamwood_no_fail_over_pressure
options.description:
Vanilla overpressure resets bar to 0, if enabled will keep pressure level within acceptable levels 
effectively making it have no failure state after letting pressure rise

options.name: steamwood_elevator_logic
options.description:
Adjust the logic for the elevator
Vanilla - No adjustments
Patient - Elevator will wait until you step on it before proceeding to the next floor (may need to cycle up and down first)
Troll - Elevator will wait until you step on it before proceeding downwards (may need to cycle up and down first)

options.name: steamwood_color_accessibility
options.description:
Adjust the color of the pressure gauge
Vanilla - No adjustments
High Contrast - black and white

options.name: aqualin_timer
options.description:
Modify how fast the 12 hour timer for collecting Aqualin ticks down
1 -> 1% of the vanilla time (roughly IRL time)
100 -> 100% vanilla
150 -> 150% timer ticks down 50% faster

options.name: trap_percent
options.description: What percent of filler should be replaced with traps.

options.name: trap_weights
options.description:
Specify the weighted chance of rolling individual trap items.

    You can use a weight of 0 to guarantee a particular trap will never appear.
        Bald Trap - Musashi's scalpe is skin colored until next area
        Camera Manipulation Trap - randomly spin/zoom the camera to a new angle
        Depression Trap - Assimilate Depress
        Sinking Trap - sink into the ground (only works for areas with water)
        Ghost Trap - Assimilate Sublime
        Instant Death Trap - instantly goes to the game over screen
        Pea Soup Trap - fill inventory with pea soup
        Poison Trap" - become poisoned
        Random Ability Trap - gain a random assimilation
        Sleepy Trap - Assimilate Sleepy
        Stinky Trap - B.O.
        Toxin Trap - Assimilate Toxin

options.name: restaurant_teleport_maze_no_fail
options.description:
Vanilla logic for picking the wrong teleporter is to send Musashi back to the start or much earlier part of the dungeon
Enabling this makes picking the wrong teleport send Musashi back to the start of the same room until the correct teleport is selected

options.name: church_fight_time_modifier
options.description:
Modify how fast the day timer progresses during the church fight
50 -> 50% time progresses at half speed
100 -> 100% vanilla
600 -> 600% time progresses 6x faster than vanilla (may still need to move to not lose)

options.name: skip_minigame_town_on_fire
options.description: Skip over the minigame to extinguish the town's fire with water scroll

options.name: skip_to_frozen_palace
options.description: If enabled, after reaching Frozen Palace for the first time, Musashi can skip Meandering Forest Maze by going up on the first screen to get to Frozen Palace

options.name: skip_minigame_ant_gondola
options.description: Skip over the minigame to get through the mines to reach the Wind Crest Guardian (still need to turn on the power and hop in the basket)

options.name: skip_over_calendar_maze
options.description: If enabled, skip over the rotating door maze in Soda Fountain where Musashi needs to reference the calendar to find the right path (loading a save might still bring you to the begining of the maze)

options.name: topo_dance_battle_logic
options.description:
Adjust the logic the pattern used by Topo
Vanilla - No adjustments
Simple - Easy pattern that is just clockwise or counter-clockwise
Random - A completely random sequence will be generated on entering room

options.name: soda_fountain_boss_rush
options.description:
If enabled, skip over all areas that are not a boss fight (no extra healing from chests or enemy drops)
cutscene intro -> Ben Fight -> Ed Fight -> Topo Fight? -> ToD -> DL
don't mash through cutscene or it wont apply the fix to connect to Ben

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: trap_link
options.description: If other games with traplink are connected to the multiworld traps are triggered in all

options.name: fast_walk
options.description: Hold L1 to go super fast (while on solid ground)

options.name: message_level
options.description:
Select how many messages appear in the bizhawk client

OFF  : Nearly no messages
ON   : Messages acknowledging a receieved item, reconnection sync messages, some warnings
HINT : Notes what action is expected of the player for the current game state in town and all the messages from ON

options.name: hair_color_selection
options.description: Pick Hair Color, or choose custom for further customization

options.name: custom_hair_color_selection
options.description: Must select custom from hair color, write hex code of desired color in the format RRGGBB (see https://www.color-hex.com/ for assistance) do not include # must be exactly 6 digits and a valid hex code i.e. 0 through F values only

options.name: scalp_color_selection

options.name: custom_scalp_color_selection
options.description: Must select custom from scalp color, write hex code of desired color in the format RRGGBB (see https://www.color-hex.com/ for assistance) do not include # must be exactly 6 digits and a valid hex code i.e. 0 through F values only
